overall_summary: The Michigan Attorney General submits reply comments aligning with NASUCA's positions on jurisdictional concerns and the inappropriateness of expedited rulemaking action. The filing also endorses the multi-state attorneys general coalition's call for FERC to prioritize ratepayer protections throughout this proceeding.

quotes.text: The Attorney General replies to express support for NASUCA's comments as to jurisdictional concerns presented by the ANOPR's arguments and potential impact.
quotes.concern: Supports NASUCA's jurisdictional objections to the ANOPR

quotes.text: The Attorney General further replies to express support for NASUCA's comments as to why expedited action would be inappropriate here.

The Attorney General also replies to express support for the request raised in comment by a group of several states' attorneys general and ratepayer advocates for FERC to "meaningfully prioritize ratepayer protections during this rulemaking process."
quotes.concern: Calls for FERC to put ratepayer protection at the center of this rulemaking

bins.name: ANOPR jurisdictional concerns
bins.description: The Michigan AG supports NASUCA's view that the ANOPR raises jurisdictional concerns about the scope of DOE's authority and its potential impact on existing FERC frameworks.

bins.name: Against expedited rulemaking
bins.description: The Michigan AG endorses NASUCA's position that expedited action on the ANOPR is inappropriate, implying the proceeding warrants more deliberate consideration.

bins.name: Ratepayer protection priority
bins.description: The Michigan AG joins a multi-state coalition urging FERC to make ratepayer protection a central priority throughout this rulemaking, not an afterthought.

bins.name: Cost protection for ratepayers
bins.description: By endorsing the call to prioritize ratepayer protections, the Michigan AG signals concern that cost allocation rules for large load interconnection must guard against shifting costs to existing customers.
